outcome_contract.why: Right now, if work gets stuck in the queue or errors start piling up, nobody knows until someone manually runs a report. This means problems can sit unnoticed for hours or days. Live monitoring would flag issues as they happen, so they get fixed before they snowball.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: Workflow queue health is continuously monitored with automatic alerts when thresholds are breached.

engineering_coverage.applicable: Required: 3 exit codes (0=healthy/warning, 1=alert, 2=error) + structured JSON per metric
N/A: reads local files only, no auth boundary
Required: stdout output serves as audit trail; optional `--output` flag for file persistence
Required: unit tests for orchestration + exit code semantics
Required: explicit file-existence checks before calling metrics-runner (which silently returns `[]` on missing files); handle large queue-state.json + telemetry.jsonl
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Health check owns the error detection layer

engineering_coverage.applicable: N/A: additive module, rollback = remove pnpm script

stage_payload.summary: Add a CLI health check that evaluates workflow queue health by calling existing `runMetricsRollup()` (queue/cycle metrics with alerts) and `summarizeWorkflowStepTelemetry()` (workflow-step metrics). The CLI outputs structured JSON to stdout, exits with code 0 (healthy), 1 (alerts present), or 2 (error), and includes a pnpm script entry for cron scheduling. This is a thin wrapper (~150-200 lines) over existing, tested infrastructure. Note: the health check is useful once cycle telemetry snapshots exist in the telemetry JSONL; when no cycle data is present, the metrics-runner returns `ready: fal
goals: Surface queue health alerts automatically instead of manual report invocation
Reuse existing metrics-runner and telemetry-summary infrastructure
Provide cron-friendly exit codes for automated monitoring
constraints: Must not mutate source data files (queue-state.json, telemetry.jsonl)
Output to stdout primary; optional gitignored file for audit trail
assumptions: Existing alert thresholds are correct and reusable
Current telemetry JSONL size is manageable for full reads
selected_approach: New `workflow-health-check.ts` CLI module calling two existing public functions, outputting structured JSON with exit code semantics
Why planning is not reopening option selection:
Analysis decisively chose Option A with clear rationale; no operator questions remain

task_briefs.title: Implement health check function and CLI
Add unit tests for health check
